mandate: Audit all internal pages for mobile usability issues and fix everything broken on mobile viewports (320px-428px)
keyFindings: HomeView had hardcoded 48px padding, 4-column stats grid, hero card with 2-column grid (image overflows on mobile), and no responsive awareness
CircleView and SettingsView had hardcoded 48px padding and 2-column form grids that overflowed on mobile
Onboarding tone selector was grid-cols-3 (too narrow on mobile), Step 8 invite form was grid-cols-2
Dashboard hamburger button had only 30px touch target (below 44px minimum)
JoinClient card had 48px padding eating into usable space on small screens
Toast notifications were fixed to right:24 which left no breathing room on narrow viewports
whatWorked: Adding useEffect-based matchMedia detection within each component (same pattern as GuestsView and InboxView already use), then using isMobile to conditionally adjust inline styles. For Tailwind-based files (onboarding), responsive prefixes (sm:grid-cols-3) are cleaner.
whatFailed: Initially tried useState with callback initializer instead of useEffect - that pattern does not work in React for side effects. Caught and corrected immediately.
recommendationsForNextAgent: The dashboard components all use inline styles with matchMedia detection. If a CSS refactor ever happens, extracting these into Tailwind responsive classes would reduce duplication. The portal and join pages are already mobile-first and need no further work. Pricing page uses Tailwind responsive correctly already.
mannaConsumed: moderate - read 10 files, made 20 targeted edits
spiritContribution: Ensured the core product experience (dashboard, onboarding, circle management) is usable on the phone screens where wedding party members will actually interact with it
